result.summary: Snap exposes four documented HTTP developer surfaces under Snapchat’s developer program: the authenticated Marketing API, authenticated Conversions API, authenticated allowlisted Public Profile API, and a public Ads Gallery API; no public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface was found for snap.com endpoints.
result.description: Snap is the company behind Snapchat and related AR/developer products such as Lens Studio, Camera Kit, and marketing tools. Its public developer documentation includes APIs for advertising, conversion event ingestion, public profile management, and ad library access.

snap_oauth_app.label: Snap OAuth app (client ID + client secret)
snap_oauth_app.setup: In [Snap Business Manager](https://business.snapchat.com/), go to the Business Details section and create an OAuth app. You must be an Organization Admin, agree to the [Snap Developer Terms](https://www.snap.com/terms/developer) and [Snap Business Tools Terms](https://www.snap.com/terms/business-services), provide a name and `redirect_uri`, and save the displayed `client_id` and `client_secret`. For Public Profile API access, after creating the app, send the `client_id` to your Snap contact to be allowlisted as described in the [Public Profile API getting started guide](https://developers.snap.com/marketing-api/Public-Profile-API/GetStarted).

variables.name: PIXEL_ID
variables.description: Pixel identifier used for web events; docs also describe replacing this with a Snap App ID for app events.

variables.name: SNAP_APP_ID
variables.description: Alternative path identifier for app events at the same `/v3/{SNAP_APP_ID}/events` pattern.
